title: Angular CLI
content:
### What is the Angular CLI?

The Angular CLI (`ng`) is a command-line tool for scaffolding, building, testing, and deploying Angular applications.

### Installation

```bash
npm install -g @angular/cli
ng version
```

### ng generate — Scaffolding

Create application artifacts with `ng generate` or the shorthand `ng g`:

| Command | Shorthand | Generated File |
|---------|-----------|----------------|
| `ng generate component my-comp` | `ng g c my-comp` | Component (TS, HTML, CSS, spec) |
| `ng generate directive my-dir` | `ng g d my-dir` | Directive |
| `ng generate pipe my-pipe` | `ng g p my-pipe` | Pipe |
| `ng generate service my-svc` | `ng g s my-svc` | Service |
| `ng generate module my-mod` | `ng g m my-mod` | Module |
| `ng generate guard my-guard` | `ng g g my-guard` | Guard |
| `ng generate interceptor my-int` | `ng g interceptor my-int` | Interceptor |
| `ng generate class my-class` | `ng g cl my-class` | Class |
| `ng generate interface my-iface` | `ng g i my-iface` | Interface |
| `ng generate enum my-enum` | `ng g e my-enum` | Enum |

### ng build

Build the application for production or development:

```bash
ng build                        # Development build
ng build --configuration production  # Production build with optimizations
ng build --output-path docs     # Custom output directory
ng build --base-href /app/      # Set base href
```

### ng serve

Start the development server with hot reload:

```bash
ng serve                        # Default port 4200
ng serve --port 4201
ng serve --open                 # Auto-open browser
ng serve --hmr                  # Hot Module Replacement
```

### ng test and ng e2e

```bash
ng test                         # Run unit tests with Karma
ng test --watch=false           # Single run (for CI)
ng test --code-coverage         # Generate coverage report
ng e2e                          # Run end-to-end tests
```

### angular.json Configuration

The `angular.json` file controls build, test, and serve options:

```json
{
  "projects": {
    "my-app": {
      "architect": {
        "build": {
          "builder": "@angular-devkit/build-angular:browser",
          "options": {
            "outputPath": "dist/my-app",
            "index": "src/index.html",
            "main": "src/main.ts",
            "polyfills": ["zone.js"],
            "tsConfig": "tsconfig.app.json",
            "assets": ["src/favicon.ico", "src/assets"],
            "styles": ["src/styles.css"],
            "scripts": []
          },
          "configurations": {
            "production": {
              "budgets": [
                { "type": "initial", "maximumWarning": "500kb", "maximumError": "1mb" }
              ],
              "outputHashing": "all",
              "optimization": true,
              "sourceMap": false
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### ng add

Add libraries to your project with automatic configuration:

```bash
ng add @angular/material       # Angular Material
ng add @angular/pwa            # PWA support
ng add @angular/ssr            # Server-side rendering
```

### ng update

Update Angular and its dependencies:

```bash
ng update @angular/core @angular/cli
ng update @angular/core --allow-dirty --create-commits
```
codeExample:
ng new my-app --standalone --routing --style=css
cd my-app
ng generate component dashboard
ng generate service api/data
ng generate module admin --route admin --module app-routing
ng serve --open
